answer.text: Two or more swing highs (resistance) or swing lows (support), extended into the future
Only the closing prices of the last 5 days
The opening prices of each month

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Upward trendlines connect higher lows (support). Downward trendlines connect lower highs (resistance). At least 2 points needed, 3+ increases reliability. **The Trap:** Trendlines must connect meaningful swing points, not random prices. More touches = more significant trendline. **Cross-Exam Context:** Ch 15 NISM XV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The trendline should be adjusted as new price data emerges. A broken trendline can signal a change in trend direction.
